hard.next_step: Set up a 60-90 minute solution workshop with Marten, potentially his manager Johan, Casper, and solution consultant Nishita to explore relevant modules and dependencies.
hard.competitor: Status quo (100% decentralized procurement, local tools, Scanmarket for eSourcing)

north_star_verdict.headline: Brand-new greenfield S2C deal, created 29 Jun, moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 30 Jun. Mårten Carlsson (Group Purchasing Excellence lead, primary) requested the demo himself on 18 Jun and proposed meeting times on 22 Jun; the agreed next milestone is a 60-90 minute solution workshop with Mårten, Johan Wijk (VP, Decision Maker) and a Zycus solution consultant. Close date Jun 2027 leaves a full 12-month enterprise cycle, so the deal is on pace for its stage. Watch items: buyer's last two-way reply was 22 Jun and rep follow-ups on 1-2 Jul are still awaiting a response, no economic buyer is engaged, Johan has not yet joined a conversation, Mårten is only 4 weeks into his role with stated limited influence, no business case is quantified, and the real threat is status-quo inertia across 35-40 decentralized ERP sites.
north_star_verdict.math: As of 06 Jul 2026, 358 days to close (close 29 Jun 2027). Opp created 29 Jun 2026, advanced to Qualified 30 Jun 2026 (6 days in Qualified). Relationship began 18 Jun 2026 (inbound demo request); buyer's last genuine two-way exchange 22 Jun 2026 (14 days ago); rep-side follow-ups 01-02 Jul awaiting reply. For a Jun 2027 close the enterprise motion allows roughly 30 days per major milestone across a 12-month cycle: discovery underway, solution workshop is the correct next milestone for Qualified. No forward slip (deal is 7 days old). Stage velocity appropriate for stage.
evidence: Mårten Carlsson requested the demo himself (Task 18 Jun 2026, 'requesting a demo') and proposed times (Task 22 Jun 2026, 'my calendar is flexible during Monday and Tuesday') — genuine inbound pull
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified on 30 Jun 2026 (field history, by Rashi Varma)
Next_Step__c names a 60-90 minute solution workshop with Mårten, Johan Wijk and a Zycus solution consultant as the immediate milestone
Decision Maker Johan Wijk (VP, Group Excellence & Sustainability) is identified in OpportunityContactRole but has not yet engaged on any conversation
No economic buyer identified after synthesis across Contact Roles, fields and activities; budget authority likely sits with Johan but is unconfirmed
No champion secured: Mårten is the primary contact but is 4 weeks into his role with stated limited influence (prior discovery, 22 Jun 2026)
No quantified business case or metrics captured; pain is real (100% decentralized, 35-40 ERP systems, 110-120 sites, zero group tooling) but not yet costed
Primary threat is status-quo inertia, not a named external vendor
Amount is $0 and no commercial or contracting process discussed — consistent with an early Qualified deal, forecast Pipeline is defensible

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case has been captured. Mårten Carlsson described clear operational pain during discovery (100% decentralized procurement across 35-40 ERP systems and 110-120 sites, no group-level visibility, compliance tooling or contract management, 'we have nothing today, zero tools') but did not translate it into cost, cycle time, savings opportunity or ROI. On budget he said 'if the case is good enough, I think it's fine' (prior discovery, 22 Jun 2026), implying an internal business case must be built but no figures have been shared. The move is to quantify the case at the upcoming solution workshop by anchoring iContract, iSource, iRisk and Merlin to measurable outcomes (contract cycle-time reduction, sourcing-event savings, supplier-risk avoidance, Merlin negotiation ROI on tail spend); without numbers the ambition against a 35-40 ERP decentralized environment keeps progression past Qualified uncertain.
sources: Prior discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten Carlsson, qualitative pain, no quantified impact)
SF Next_Step__c (workshop planned, opportunity to build the quantified case)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is confirmed. Johan Wijk (Vice President, Group Excellence & Sustainability) is tagged Decision Maker in OpportunityContactRole and, as the VP owning group purchasing maturity and Mårten's manager, is the most likely budget owner for a group-level investment, but he has not appeared on any call or email and his commercial authority is unvalidated. Mårten signalled the budget test conversationally ('if the case is good enough, I think it's fine', 22 Jun 2026) without naming who releases funds. Because this is a brand-new Qualified deal with $0 amount and no commercial discussion, the EB is genuinely unmapped; the move is to use the workshop to confirm Johan as the budget owner (or surface whoever above him sponsors group spend) and secure his active participation.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Johan Wijk = Decision Maker, VP Group Excellence & Sustainability; not yet engaged)
Prior discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten on budget test)

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging but not formally documented. From discovery, the buyer's implied evaluation lens is group-level standardization and visibility, compliance and supplier-risk control across a fragmented estate, and AI/automation that local tools and Scanmarket cannot provide, with feasibility of a phased rollout that does not demand immediate integration of 35-40 ERPs. These are inferred from the stated pain rather than a confirmed requirements list, and no RFI/RFP, ShoeFit or BRD round has run yet. The workshop is the moment to convert this into explicit, ranked criteria (which modules first, integration tolerance, quick-win definition) so the evaluation has a scorecard we shaped.
sources: Prior discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten, decentralized pain and stack)
SF Products__c (iContract, iSource, iRisk, Merlin suite in scope)

decision_process.narrative: The decision process has not been mapped. The only structured next step on record is the planned solution workshop with Mårten, Johan and a Zycus solution consultant; no evaluation stages, approval chain, procurement channel or timeline have been described, and SF activities include an explicit 'Get clarity on the evaluation stages and influence the process' health flag (30 Jun 2026). The call sequence so far is thin (an inbound demo request 18 Jun, a scheduling exchange 22 Jun, rep follow-ups 01-02 Jul) so there is no join/escalation pattern to read yet. The move is to use the workshop to establish who evaluates, who approves group spend, and the buyer's intended decision timeline.
sources: SF Task 30 Jun 2026 ('Get clarity on the evaluation stages and influence the process')

paper_process.narrative: No paper or contracting mechanics have been discussed. MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel and ERP/systems-integration scope have not been raised, and no SI or partner is involved. Given a 35-40 ERP, 110-120 site estate, integration scoping and InfoSec review will eventually be material, but at Qualified with $0 amount this is expected rather than a red flag. Visibility is created later, once module scope and a business case are locked; the workshop should surface the buyer's procurement and legal gatekeepers as a follow-on.
sources: No evidence in SF fields, activities or calls this sweep

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is real, greenfield and buyer-owned. Mårten Carlsson stated 'we have nothing today, zero tools' for group-level procurement and described 100% decentralized procurement across 35-40 ERP systems and 110-120 sites with no central visibility, compliance tooling or contract management, local buyers using their own tools (Scanmarket for eSourcing at some sites) with no standardization, risk management or AI/automation (prior discovery, 22 Jun 2026). The pain owners are Mårten (Group Purchasing Excellence lead) and Johan Wijk (VP Group Excellence & Sustainability), whose mandate is to drive purchasing maturity and compliance across the group. It is confirmed and operational; the only gap is that it has not been quantified into financial impact.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Mårten and Johan as pain owners per titles/mandate)

champion.narrative: No champion is secured. Mårten Carlsson (Group Purchasing Excellence lead, primary contact) is genuinely engaged — he requested the demo, led discovery, asked detailed questions and proposed meeting times — but he explicitly stated he is 4 weeks into his role with limited influence (prior discovery, 22 Jun 2026) and has not yet demonstrated advocacy: no introductions to an economic buyer, no internal selling, no pushing back on alternatives, and he has not yet opened active access to Johan. His engagement is active but his champion strength is weak on tenure and political capital. Johan Wijk (his manager, the Decision Maker) is expected at the workshop but has not engaged. The move is to develop Mårten by arming him with a quantified group-level business case he can carry internally, and to convert Johan into an executive sponsor at the same session.
sources: Prior discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: '4 weeks in role, limited influence')
SF OpportunityContactRole (Mårten Evaluator/primary; Johan Decision Maker, not engaged)

competition.narrative: The primary competitor is the status quo: 100% decentralized procurement with local tools, no group-level solution, and Scanmarket for eSourcing at some sites where users are satisfied, which lowers urgency for a group replacement. Mårten also raised internal development but conceded 'we don't have the expertise to build procurement tools in-house' (prior discovery, 22 Jun 2026). No external vendor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) has been named in any source this sweep, and none is asserted. The competitive risk is inertia: if the workshop fails to prove feasibility of a phased quick win, the buyer defaults to decentralized local tools. The wedge is Merlin (AI negotiation), iRisk (supplier-risk visibility) and group-level compliance/visibility that neither local tools nor internal development deliver; activation is a phased roadmap (an iRisk or iContract quick win first, full suite later) that avoids immediate 35-ERP integration.
sources: Prior discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: 'we have nothing today, zero tools'; Scanmarket; internal-build option)
Living memory: status quo, internal development

deal_movement.summary: Deal is 7 days old and moving at stage pace. Relationship opened with Mårten's inbound demo request (18 Jun), a scheduling exchange (22 Jun), opp created and re-owned to Casper Hoeholt (29 Jun), stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified (30 Jun), Division moved Zycus to New Markets (29 Jun). Since 22 Jun the buyer has been quiet while the rep follows up (introduction follow-up 01 Jul, share discovery slides 02 Jul) — rep-side touches awaiting a buyer reply. The live next step is confirming the solution workshop.

competitive_position.summary: The field is not a vendor bake-off, it is a fight against inertia. The single strongest current threat is the status quo: 100% decentralized procurement with local tools and Scanmarket eSourcing that some sites are happy with, so do-nothing is the default (prior discovery, 22 Jun 2026). Internal build was floated but self-ruled-out on capability ('we don't have the expertise'). No external SaaS vendor (Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, Ivalua) has surfaced in any source and none is asserted. We win by proving a phased quick win and leading with Merlin AI and iRisk, which neither local tools nor an internal build can match.

competitors.quote: We have nothing today, zero tools. Some sites use Scanmarket for eSourcing and are satisfied with it.
competitors.how_we_win: Prove a phased Q4 quick win (iRisk or iContract) that delivers group visibility without immediate 35-ERP integration, so doing nothing costs more than starting.

competitors.name: Internal development (build in-house)
competitors.quote: We don't have the expertise to build procurement tools in-house.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin negotiation AI and iRisk supplier-risk depth that an in-house team cannot build or maintain.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expects a group-level answer to a fully decentralized estate, delivered in a way that is feasible without boiling the ocean on 35-40 ERPs. Fit is strong on capability breadth (S2C suite plus Merlin AI and iRisk) but exposed on proof of phased feasibility and a quantified case.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for an early, greenfield, single-threaded deal: arm the new champion, get the Decision Maker in the room, and beat inertia with a phased quick win.
flags: Arm Mårten (4 weeks in role, limited influence) with a quantified group-level business case he can carry to Johan — he cannot mandate adoption alone.
Break single-thread: secure Johan Wijk (VP, Decision Maker) actively at the workshop to validate budget authority and sponsorship.
Neutralize status-quo inertia by proving a phased Q4 quick win (iRisk or iContract) that avoids up-front 35-40 ERP integration.

champion_strength.summary: No champion secured. Mårten Carlsson is the primary contact and is actively engaged (requested the demo himself, led discovery, proposed meeting times, committed to mapping contacts), but he explicitly stated he is 4 weeks into his role with limited influence and has shown no advocacy yet: no executive introductions, no internal selling, no pushing back on the status quo. What has developed: genuine inbound interest and willingness to run a workshop. What is missing: political capital, proven advocacy, and access to the Decision Maker/economic buyer. Develop him by handing him a quantified business case for the workshop and convert Johan Wijk into an executive sponsor.
champion_strength.source: Prior discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: '4 weeks in role, limited influence') + SF OpportunityContactRole

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is a natural wedge here: Merlin, Merlin Intake and ML iAnalyze are in the product scope and neither the decentralized local tools nor an internal build can deliver AI negotiation or supplier-risk intelligence. But the Merlin/I2O narrative has not yet been put in front of the buyer, so we are under-positioned on our sharpest differentiator. Make Merlin the centerpiece of the workshop.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field not populated on this record. Product scope is AI-heavy (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ML iAnalyze), and the greenfield, no-tools state means AI/automation is a clear leap the buyer has not yet articulated appetite for. Treat as curious until the workshop tests appetite directly.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Brand-new Swedish manufacturing S2C deal (created 29 Jun) with a genuinely interested but 4-week-tenure primary contact and no economic buyer engaged; the win hinges on locking the solution workshop and arming Mårten with a quantified group-level case before status-quo inertia reasserts.

win_position.text: We are early but genuinely in the game: Mårten Carlsson requested the demo himself on 18 Jun and Trelleborg runs 100% decentralized procurement with zero group tooling, so the pain is real and the field is greenfield with no external vendor competing.
No economic buyer is engaged and Decision Maker Johan Wijk (VP, Group Excellence & Sustainability) has not joined a single conversation, so there is no proven path to a group-level budget yet.

Our only contact, Mårten, is 4 weeks into his role and stated limited influence, and the real threat is do-nothing inertia across 35-40 decentralized ERP sites rather than a rival we can out-position.

deal_momentum.text: Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified on 30 Jun and the agreed next step is a 60-90 minute solution workshop with Mårten, Johan and a Zycus solution consultant.
The buyer's last two-way reply was 22 Jun and rep follow-ups on 1 and 2 Jul are still unanswered, so the workshop is not yet on the calendar and a cold-flag has already fired.

customer_commitment.text: Mårten proposed meeting times himself on 22 Jun ('my calendar is flexible Monday and Tuesday') and committed to mapping internal contacts, an early pull signal.
No date, budget or written business case is committed, the deal amount is still $0, and no procurement or contracting process has been discussed.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded to one new, low-influence contact with no executive sponsor and no quantified ROI, against a decentralized organization prone to status-quo inertia.
A delayed or missed workshop lets the greenfield urgency fade back into 'we have nothing today and it's fine,' turning this into a no-decision risk rather than a competitive loss.
